sections.paragraph:
pl. בְּאֵרֹות const. בֶּאֱרֹור ( Gen 14:10), f.

(1) a well (Arab. بءِْيُر, Syr. ܒܺܐܪܐܳ, ܒܺܪܐܳ, id.), Gen 24:11, 20 Gen 24:20 26:19 , 20 Gen 26:20, 21 Gen 26:21, etc.; often more fully בְּאֵר מַיִם, Gen 21:19 בְּאֵר מַיִם חַיִּים, 26:19 . It is distinguished from a fountain (עַיִן) on the surface of the ground or flowing from a rock; a well (בְּאֵר) may however be also called a fountain (עַיִן), see Gen 16:7, comp. ver. 14 Gen 16:14 24:11 , 13 Gen 24:13, 16 Gen 24:16. Used of wells of bitumen; Gen 14:10.

(2) a pit, Ps. 55:24 69:16 .

(3) [Beer], pr.n.-(a) of a station of the Israelites on the borders of Moab; -Num. 21:1618 , prob. the same place which in Isa 15:8, is more fully called בְּאֵר אֵלִים [Beer-elim], “the well of heroes.”-(b) a town of Palestine, Jud. 9:21 [see Rob. ii. 132].
